@davitausberlin @davitausberlin Great feedback — the product sourcing angle is super interesting.

I’m trying to understand where the first group of users would come from.

My current hypothesis is:

 teams already running internal agents (procurement / research)
 builders with personal agents
 or people doing AEO / agent visibility

Does that match what you’re seeing? Or are there specific communities / use cases where this would be more natural?

Tasos V

# Replit's AI Agent Website Builder - Honest Review

by

chatWise

chatWise

•

The Agent is good, if you want to build something insanely basic. Like an EdTech landing page, or an one pager for validating an idea. For anything else, which is probably what everyone needs is super buggy. I tried to build an AI Agent that manages private keys and signs transactions for users. Replit for some reason created an app with the following things 1. Page to login/register 2. Page to generate my Keys 3. An OpenAI integration where I ask where should I store my keys and gives me an answer LOLOLOL . AI Replacing developers? NOT THERE YET.

3.3K views

### Replies

Best

Rajiv Ayyangar

Product Hunt

Product Hunt [...] Report

Shane Pollard

I agree that it is not there yet, but that could change 3 months from now. I see replit ship changes FAST. Those guys move at pace. I like the simplicity of UI its familiar. The opportunity for everyone to build something just got easier.

Report

Prithvi Damera

Replit Agent is an AI-powered tool on Replit Core or Teams that helps users build software from natural language prompts. It assists with coding, debugging, and deployment, making development accessible but may have limitations and require detailed prompts.

Report

Subscribe [...] Report

Tasos V

chatWise

chatWise

@whatshivamdo yeah it is super new. they launched it for free recently. Before it was paid, an agent to kick off a repo, but now is a website builder one. At least it tries to lol

Report

steve beyatte

Product Hunt

Product Hunt

There must be great examples of Replit agent building simple stuff, can anyone share any?

Report

Sanskar Yadav

Replit’s agent feels more like boilerplate on autopilot.   
  
As a non-coder, I found it for quick prototypes, but still too clunky for complex builds.

I'm quite curious, do devs see it becoming a true co‑pilot soon?

Report

Shane Pollard

results.title: Lemni: Set up custom Ai agents for all your customer interactions | Product Hunt
results.content:
Adapt

What do you think? …

Krijn Rijshouwer
Lemni

Lemni

Andres Vlaeminck
Revealio: Discover & Connect

Revealio: Discover & Connect

Great product!  
How do you handle long tasks; and can the agents follow up with you after say 2 weeks?  
  
Great job!

Krijn Rijshouwer
Lemni

Lemni

@andres\_vlaeminck Good question! We actually have a scheduling tool on the roadmap. So sometime soon they should indeed also allow you to set up follow up tasks.

Sam @CRANQ [...] Rijk van Wel

This is awesome. Great value proposition, and clear simple pricing ($0.04 per customer interaction). As an indie builder, I would use this over something like Help Scout. It offers users support that doesn’t feel like a chatbot, while taking up labor intensive tasks like issuing partial refunds. I’ll give this a try on the project I’m currently working on.

Joost van Hoof
Freddy Feedback

Freddy Feedback

Very exciting to see AI agents getting out of the technical context and into the hands of a much bigger audience. This is the future!

James Whitehall

Well done on launch. As a founder I know how much work you have put into this. My startup is not quite ready for this tool yet, but I have bookmarked it. I can see its valuew

Krijn Rijshouwer

results.title: The best AI agents in 2026 | Product Hunt
results.content:
NextLast

## Frequently asked questions about AI Agents

Real answers from real users, pulled straight from launch discussions, forums, and reviews.

 ### Q: Can AI agents be sandboxed in the browser without local file access?

  Happycapy shows it’s possible to run agent-like apps without relying on local files: reviewers praise its built-in AI, database, and analytics that let you build and launch agents that operate on cloud data. In practice, sandboxing in the browser without local file access means the platform must host the agent runtime, use cloud storage/APIs, and surface a secure in‑browser UI.

  Key points: [...] ## Top reviewed AI agents

Top reviewed

"Across the top-reviewed AI Agents products, voice and customer-facing automation dominate: ElevenLabs and Vapi stand out for low-latency speech, multilingual voice workflows, and developer-friendly APIs for real-time calling, transcription, and conversational agents. Elsewhere, tools skew toward operational execution—support, workflow orchestration, analytics, and prototyping—favoring integrations, human handoff, visual builders, and domain-specific automation over pure chat."
